name: African Wild Dog
title: Best Friend
description: The African Wild Dog is a perseverant hunter, true social animal, and one of many competing predators in its Savannah home.
why: Dogged persistence and extreme loyalty are a few of your defining traits. You enjoy exploring ideas and conversing, but can become drained when you socialize for too long, like the wild dog’s need to back down in its dominating environment. Taking time to yourself is crucial as you battle stress, but you’re always ready when your friends call.

understandResult.title: Organization
understandResult.text: The African Wild Dog has come up with the perfect hunting strategy for each animal, leading to a high rate of success. You are organized, and come up with an attack plan before you pursue a goal.

understandResult.title: Community
understandResult.text: The most sociable dogs in the world, they live in packs of 10-30 dogs. They have even been observed exhibiting behavior similar to voting! You make decisions based on how other people will feel, and your values guide you over reason.

understandResult.title: Extroversion
understandResult.text: If a large enough predator asserts itself, they might need to back down from their prey. You need time to recharge, and can get overwhelmed in large groups.

understandResult.title: Exploration
understandResult.text: African Wild Dogs require large swaths of territory to maintain their packs. You enjoy exploring new ideas and possibilities.

understandResult.title: Confidence
understandResult.text: As individuals, they must be cautious as they can be picked off by other predators. But as a unit, African Wild Dogs are confident and powerful. You are resilient, but may struggle with confidence from time to time.

facts.Physical Description: Their fur is red, black, white, brown and yellow in a random pattern, leading to the nickname “painted dog.” They have four toes on each paw, and round, perky ears.
facts.Is it Predator or Prey?: African Wild Dogs hunt in a pack, and their targets are usually much faster and larger. As a result, they are known for their dogged persistence in chasing after their prey until the animal is too tired to continue. Then, they corner the animal as a pack. Wild Dogs have a distinct hunting strategy for each type of prey, leading to an estimated success rate between 60-90%. Examples of those prey consist of antelope, warthog, and wildebeest.
facts.Community and Trust: The most sociable dogs in the world, their packs have strict ranking systems, led by the dominant breeding pair. Within the pack, they raise their young, care for their sick, and eat together. Packs are composed of 10-30 individuals. Some scientists have even observed behavior akin to "voting," through the use of sneezes when the pack rallies!
facts.Is it Assertive: These dogs must coexist with a wide variety of other predators and dangerous prey. Their small size relative to other predators requires them to abandon some prey if a large enough competitor asserts itself. Adaptability in situations with others is an important part of their survival.
facts.Does it Migrate?: Since African Wild Dogs maintain some territory but require large expanses to maintain their packs, they roam large areas of their Savannah homes.
facts.Where is it on the Food Chain?: As individuals, they must be cautious as they can be picked off by other predators. But as a unit, African Wild Dogs are confident and powerful.
facts.Endangered Status: Habitat destruction and human intervention have led the African Wild Dog to critically endangered status by the IUCN Red List for all of its subpopulations. Like many other African species, stronger protections for their savannah homes will make all the difference in these dogs’ future.

charityDesc: The African Wildlife Foundation is based in Africa. Its focus on collaborating with local organizations makes them uniquely equipped to drive change.
